items.title: BBVA puts AI at the core of banking with OpenAI
items.summary: BBVA is centering its banking strategy around artificial intelligence through a major partnership with OpenAI. The company has successfully scaled ChatGPT Enterprise for use across 100,000 employees. This collaboration aims to significantly advance the bank's global, AI-powered transformation efforts. Specific focus areas include integrating advanced AI capabilities throughout banking operations worldwide.
items.audio_script: From OpenAI - News, "BBVA puts AI at the core of banking with OpenAI." The report details a significant strategic pivot by BBVA to center its entire global banking operation around artificial intelligence capabilities through a major partnership with OpenAI. Specifically, the bank has successfully scaled ChatGPT Enterprise for deployment across its entire workforce of 100,000 employees. This extensive collaboration marks a key milestone in advancing the bank's worldwide, AI-powered transformation objectives by integrating advanced AI tools into numerous operational facets globally. Industry observers suggest this move signals a growing trend of major financial institutions adopting large language models for foundational enterprise integration across various sectors.

items.title: A Coding Implementation on Microsoft SkillOpt for Instrumented Prompt Optimization, Skill Evolution Analysis, and Baseline Comparison
items.summary: An instrumented workflow was implemented to conduct an end-to-end process on Microsoft SkillOpt. The setup involved configuring the repository, connecting OpenAI-compatible model access, and setting up optimizers and target models. A real optimization loop incorporating rollout, reflection, aggregation, selection, updating, and validation gating was executed. Training history, accuracy visualization, edit-budget behavior, and token usage were inspected during this process. Finally, the evolved skill's performance was compared against the original seed skill acting as a baseline.
items.audio_script: From MarkTechPost, "A Coding Implementation on Microsoft SkillOpt for Instrumented Prompt Optimization, Skill Evolution Analysis, and Baseline Comparison." This report details a technical implementation demonstrating a structured method for optimizing artificial intelligence skills using established platform tools. The process involved establishing an instrumented workflow on Microsoft SkillOpt, which required configuring the repository and integrating access to OpenAI-compatible models. Researchers executed a full optimization loop encompassing rollout, reflection, aggregation, selection, updating, and validation gating. Key metrics observed throughout this cycle included training history, accuracy visualization, edit-budget behavior, and token utilization. The final performance metrics of the enhanced skill were subsequently contrasted directly against the initial seed skill serving as the control baseline. This development represents a procedural advance for automated AI refinement, suggesting standardized methodologies for verifiable model improvement within complex systems.

items.title: How to Refactor Code with Claude Code
items.summary: Details how to boost coding agent productivity through refactoring techniques. The guide, originally published on Towards Data Science, focuses on improving existing codebases using Claude Code's capabilities. Specific methods for restructuring and enhancing code are provided. By following the advice, developers can create more efficient and maintainable software.
items.audio_script: From Towards Data Science, "How to Refactor Code with Claude Code." The article details methods for enhancing coding agent productivity specifically through the process of code refactoring. This guide outlines several structured techniques developers can implement to improve existing software structures using the capabilities of Claude Code. These approaches involve systematic restructuring and enhancement of established codebases, leading to more efficient and maintainable final products. Developers following these outlined procedures are advised on creating cleaner application logic. The insights presented suggest a measurable increase in efficiency when integrating advanced LLMs into routine development workflows across various technology sectors.

items.title: Labor to set terms for datacentre and AI growth as it vows not to repeat mistakes of resources boom
items.summary: Andrew Charlton, assistant minister for the digital economy, stated Australia must set the terms for the emerging AI and datacentre boom. He acknowledged legitimate concerns regarding resource consumption associated with this growth wave. The rate of expansion in datacentres has been explosive over the last two years. In New South Wales alone, there are 44 pipeline projects collectively seeking 11GW of electricity capacity. Charlton urged Australia to avoid repeating the mistakes experienced during past resources booms by proactively managing this new industrial surge.
items.audio_script: From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "Labor to set terms for datacentre and AI growth as it vows not to repeat mistakes of resources boom." This reporting focuses on government intentions regarding the rapidly expanding artificial intelligence and data centre infrastructure sector in Australia. Assistant Minister for the Digital Economy, Andrew Charlton, asserted that appropriate regulatory frameworks must be established to govern this technological expansion. The discussion highlighted explosive recent increases in datacentre development, citing New South Wales alone with 44 pipeline projects collectively requesting an estimated eleven gigawatts of electricity capacity. Officials stressed the necessity of proactive management to circumvent pitfalls observed during previous resource boom cycles. Attention will now focus on regulatory mechanisms implemented for this accelerated growth phase within the digital economy.

items.title: Supporting Europe’s work in ensuring a trustworthy AI ecosystem
items.summary: OpenAI endorses the EU Code of Practice concerning AI content transparency. This support focuses on promoting robust provenance standards for AI outputs. Such standards aim to provide users with better understanding of machine-generated material. By supporting these efforts, OpenAI contributes to building a trustworthy artificial intelligence ecosystem within Europe. This commitment helps establish clarity regarding the origin of digital content.
items.audio_script: From OpenAI - News, "Supporting Europe’s work in ensuring a trustworthy AI ecosystem." The announcement details OpenAI's endorsement of the European Union Code of Practice regarding artificial intelligence content transparency, signaling increased industry alignment with provenance standards. This support centers on promoting robust frameworks designed to provide users with clearer understanding concerning machine-generated material. By participating in these collaborative efforts, the organization contributes directly to the development of a more reliable and trustworthy artificial intelligence ecosystem within Europe, thereby establishing greater clarity about digital content origins. This endorsement signals an industry movement toward standardized transparency protocols for synthetic media across international markets.

items.title: Why AI hasn’t replaced software engineers, and won’t
items.summary: Artificial intelligence has not replaced software engineers because current coding agents are still considered novel technologies. While AI can assist with coding tasks, it lacks the comprehensive understanding and adaptability of human engineers. Software engineering requires complex problem-solving that goes beyond pattern recognition. Human expertise remains crucial for architecting intricate systems and handling ambiguous requirements. Therefore, AI serves as a powerful tool augmenting human capability rather than replacing the engineer entirely.
items.audio_script: From AI Snake Oil, "Why AI hasn’t replaced software engineers, and won’t." The report addresses the current role of artificial intelligence relative to human expertise within the field of software engineering. Current coding agents are characterized as novel technologies that assist rather than supersede professional capabilities. While AI demonstrates proficiency in specific coding tasks, it reportedly lacks the holistic comprehension necessary for complex system architecture or adapting to ambiguous requirements, indicating a continued need for human ingenuity in intricate problem-solving. The findings suggest that artificial intelligence functions optimally as a potent augmentation tool supplementing existing engineering skill sets, rather than serving as a replacement mechanism.

items.title: AI absolutism is breaking our brains. The apocalyptic future we’re being sold isn’t inevitable
items.summary: Conflicting narratives surrounding artificial intelligence create mental strain, presenting both apocalyptic warnings and boundless promises of potential. The discourse forces people to confront contradictory ideas—whether embracing or abstaining from the technology. Economically, AI is already a significant force, projected to contribute nearly 60% of US economic growth by the last quarter of 2025. Experts are currently preoccupied with predicting potential calamities should an "AI bubble" burst. Overall, the overwhelming and inconsistent discussion surrounding AI suggests a narrative that may be exaggerated or incomplete.
items.audio_script: From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "AI absolutism is breaking our brains. The apocalyptic future we’re being sold isn’t inevitable." The report examines the mental strain caused by conflicting narratives surrounding artificial intelligence, concluding that much of the prevailing discourse may be exaggerated or incomplete. Detailed reporting indicated that current discussions oscillate between hyperbolic warnings of technological catastrophe and unsubstantiated promises of limitless potential. Economically, AI's impact is already substantial, projected to account for nearly sixty percent of U.S. economic growth by the final quarter of 2025. Furthermore, experts have become intensely focused on modeling potential damages should an anticipated "AI bubble" deflate. The industry will likely see a shift toward more measured analyses regarding AI's tangible integration points in established sectors.

items.title: Bayesian Networks and Markov Networks: An Intuitive Guide to Structured Uncertainty
items.summary: Offers an intuitive guide to reasoning under uncertainty using structured models. It covers a spectrum of techniques ranging from directed Bayesian networks to undirected Markov networks. The content also introduces weighted logical rules as methods for handling uncertainty. These frameworks provide ways to model complex relationships within data. Ultimately, the piece demystifies these advanced graphical models for practitioners.
items.audio_script: From Towards Data Science, "Bayesian Networks and Markov Networks: An Intuitive Guide to Structured Uncertainty." This piece provides an accessible overview of structured modeling techniques used for reasoning under uncertainty. The material details a spectrum of graphical models, spanning from directed Bayesian networks to undirected Markov networks, illustrating how they model complex data relationships. Furthermore, the guide introduces weighted logical rules as supplementary methods for handling probabilistic ambiguity. These frameworks collectively offer practitioners methods to structure and understand interdependencies within datasets. Understanding these advanced graphical models proves valuable for developing more robust artificial intelligence applications across various industrial sectors.

items.title: Those tedious errands, tasks and chores that AI wants to replace? They help keep you fit | Manoush Zomorodi and Keith Diaz
items.summary: Excessive convenience, driven partly by AI advancement, risks undermining human physical health. While technology automates complex tasks like document review or domestic scheduling, this trend echoes past innovations that did not improve fitness levels. Examples such as microwaves replacing cooking or escalators displacing stairs illustrate this pattern of reduced activity. Historically, labor-saving technologies have rarely prompted healthier behaviors in people. The ease of modern digital "taps" risks eroding the necessity and habit of physical effort.
items.audio_script: From The Guardian - Artificial Intelligence, "Those tedious errands, tasks and chores that AI wants to replace? They help keep you fit | Manoush Zomorodi and Keith Diaz." This report suggests that the push for technological convenience through artificial intelligence may inadvertently undermine human physical fitness. The discussion notes that while technology automates complex processes, this trend parallels previous innovations which have not necessarily improved levels of physical activity. Examples cited include the advent of microwaves replacing traditional cooking methods or escalators substituting stairs in use, illustrating a recurring pattern of reduced manual effort correlated with technological advancement. Current digital ease risks eroding habitual physical exertion required for daily life maintenance.

items.title: asyncinject 0.7
items.summary: Introduces version 0.7 of the asyncinject utility library. This library was developed to support an asyncio dependency injection pattern in Python. The author noted that using it with Datasette and Claude Fable 5 helped reveal bugs within the dependency structure. It is characterized as a very proactive model due to this testing process.
items.audio_script: From Simon Willison's Weblog, "asyncinject 0.7." The update introduces version 0.7 of the asyncinject utility library designed to facilitate an asyncio dependency injection pattern within Python environments. Development work incorporating this new iteration was reportedly conducted with Datasette and Claude Fable 5, a combination that aided in uncovering various latent bugs within the existing dependency structure. This process has characterized the toolset as being highly proactive in its testing capabilities. The release signals continued evolution toward more robust asynchronous dependency management practices across Python-based applications.

items.title: datasette-agent 0.2a0
items.summary: Version 0.2a0 introduces a key capability allowing tools to prompt users for input during execution. Tools accepting context parameters receive a `ToolContext` object and can use `context.ask_user()` to pose questions (yes/no, multiple-choice, or free-text). When suspended awaiting an answer, the conversation persists through UI rendering and database storage. A new built-in `save_query` tool enables agents to save generated SQL as a Datasette stored query, which requires explicit user approval before execution.
items.audio_script: From Simon Willison's Weblog, "datasette-agent 0.2a0." The latest version introduces significant enhancements allowing automated tools to interactively gather user input during their operational cycles. These updates enable context parameters to receive a ToolContext object, which facilitates the use of context.ask_user() for prompting users with yes/no, multiple-choice, or free-text questions, maintaining conversation state through UI rendering and database persistence while awaiting responses. Furthermore, a new built-in save_query tool is implemented, permitting agents to store generated SQL as a Datasette stored query, necessitating explicit user approval prior to any execution. These capabilities mark a move toward more deeply interactive and controllable AI data tooling within the ecosystem.

items.title: NuCS vs Choco: A Pure-Python Constraint Solver Meets a JVM Veteran
items.summary: A performance comparison was conducted between two constraint solvers, NuCS and Choco. NuCS is described as a pure-Python solver, while Choco is characterized as having roots in the Java Virtual Machine (JVM). The content details an in-depth testing methodology comparing the capabilities of these two distinct tools. This technical assessment evaluates their performance characteristics side-by-side.
items.audio_script: From Towards Data Science, "NuCS vs Choco: A Pure-Python Constraint Solver Meets a JVM Veteran." This report details a performance comparison between two established constraint solving tools, NuCS and Choco. The testing methodology involved an in-depth, side-by-side technical assessment designed to evaluate the distinct operational characteristics of each solver. Researchers employed rigorous procedures to test the capabilities inherent in NuCS, which operates as a pure-Python solution, against Choco, which possesses roots within the Java Virtual Machine environment. The analysis produced quantifiable performance metrics for both systems across various problem sets. This comparison offers developers insights into selecting an appropriate high-performance constraint solver based on platform preference and operational requirements.

items.title: How an astrophysicist uses Codex to help simulate black holes
items.summary: Astrophysicist Chi-kwan Chan employs the tool Codex for creating detailed black hole simulations. These simulations enable scientists to examine physics under extreme conditions near black holes. Such research is vital for testing the accuracy of Einstein's general theory of relativity. By using Codex, Chan advances our understanding of these complex gravitational phenomena. The process helps push the boundaries of theoretical astrophysics.
items.audio_script: From OpenAI - News, "How an astrophysicist uses Codex to help simulate black holes." The report details how computational tools are being utilized in astrophysics research to model extreme cosmic events. Astrophysicist Chi-kwan Chan is employing the programming tool Codex to generate complex and detailed simulations of black holes. These simulations allow researchers to observe fundamental physics operating under conditions characterized by extreme gravitational forces near these objects. This rigorous examination is crucial for validating the predictive accuracy of Einstein's general theory of relativity in extreme spacetime environments. The integration of advanced AI tools with theoretical modeling advances the field’s understanding of complex gravitational phenomena, pushing established boundaries in astrophysics research methodologies.

items.title: Inside soccer’s data renaissance
items.source: MIT Technology Review - AI & Emerging Tech
items.summary: Modern soccer is undergoing a significant data-driven transformation, challenging traditional understandings of play. Advanced analytics reveal complex patterns that are invisible to casual observers. These new methods analyze everything from player movement to tactical formations with unprecedented detail. The insights gained are reshaping how coaches train and how analysts interpret game flow. Essentially, science is revealing the deeper strategies behind the sport's visible action.
items.audio_script: From MIT Technology Review - AI & Emerging Tech, "Inside soccer’s data renaissance." The report details how modern soccer is undergoing a profound transformation driven by advanced analytics, revealing complex patterns previously invisible to observers. Methodologies employed analyze player movement and tactical formations with unprecedented levels of detail. These new analytical methods generate insights that are actively reshaping both coaching regimens and the interpretation of live game flow, essentially allowing science to map deeper strategies beneath visible athletic action. This integration signals a continuing trend where data processing is becoming integral to professional sports performance analysis across multiple industries.

items.title: We’re bringing Walmart Connect to Display & Video 360.
items.summary: Walmart Connect is expanding its reach into Display & Video 360. This strategic move aims to improve how brands connect with consumers. Advertisers can now utilize the platform for running targeted YouTube campaigns. Furthermore, this integration will allow for comprehensive measurement of advertising outcomes. Overall, it provides a new channel for reaching high-intent shoppers through YouTube.
items.audio_script: From Google / Google AI (Keyword Blog), "We’re bringing Walmart Connect to Display & Video 360." This announcement details the expansion of Walmart Connect's capabilities into the advertising platform, enhancing how brands can interact with consumers across digital channels. The integration enables advertisers to deploy targeted YouTube campaigns directly through Display & Video 360, facilitating a new pathway to high-intent shoppers. Moreover, this union provides comprehensive measurement tools for analyzing advertising results within the expanded framework. Industry observers note that this development solidifies multichannel programmatic reach, influencing future platform integrations across major ad tech providers.

items.title: The Download: the “steroid olympics” and a safer Mythos
items.source: MIT Technology Review - AI & Emerging Tech
items.summary: A newsletter named "The Download" delivers daily updates on the technology world. It features commentary on cultural phenomena, using the term "steroid olympics." The piece addresses what the "steroid olympics" represented as a kind of circus reflecting current culture. Specific mention is made of an event that took place a couple of weeks prior at a $50 million arena located in a casino parking lot.
items.audio_script: From MIT Technology Review - AI & Emerging Tech, "The Download: the “steroid olympics” and a safer Mythos." The report analyzes contemporary cultural trends by referencing the concept of the "steroid Olympics," concluding that such phenomena reflect underlying societal dynamics rather than pure technological advancement. Two weeks prior, a high-profile event occurred within a structure valued at fifty million dollars, situated in a casino parking lot; details surrounding this display were noted in context with broader shifts in digital culture. The analysis suggests a move toward integrating novelty spectacle within established entertainment venues warrants continued monitoring by the technology sector.

items.title: Quoting Jeremy Howard
items.summary: Jeremy Howard suggests an easy method to slow recursive AI self-improvement: the top lab must refrain from using its best model on frontier AI while allowing others access. This hypothetical scenario would halt frontier advancement but prevent a dangerous power imbalance. He critiques Anthropic for pursuing the opposite path by reserving their best model for internal, frontier research. Howard argues that such actions cause the AI frontier to advance rapidly and increase concentrated power. Nevertheless, he advocates for democratizing open development rather than attempting to slow down improvement altogether.
items.audio_script: From Simon Willison's Weblog, "Quoting Jeremy Howard," a discussion emerged detailing strategies to regulate the pace of recursive artificial intelligence self-improvement. The central suggestion proposes a specific operational restriction for leading research laboratories concerning their most advanced AI models. This limitation involves restricting direct use of top-tier models on frontier systems while simultaneously ensuring those capabilities remain accessible to a broader developer community. Jeremy Howard criticized certain practices, citing an example where withholding superior models solely for internal development was predicted to accelerate advancement and concentrate power among a few entities. Consequently, the emphasis remains on open democratization of development methods as a primary regulatory mechanism for the sector.

items.title: From Explicit Elements to Implicit Intent: A Predefined Library for Auditable Behavioral Inference
items.summary: SemantiClean is a modular framework designed for extracting structured semantic signals from e-commerce session data to infer metrics like purchase intent and customer segmentation. It prioritizes auditability and structural governance over maximizing predictive accuracy, ensuring element-level transparency. The framework structures twenty-four behavioral elements across four layers: Functional, Interaction, Systemic, and Contextual. Signal quality is maintained using three anti-inflation mechanisms: RedundancyGroup caps, TieredPenaltyCalculator penalties, and AdaptiveConstraintMode protection. Inference is driven by a two-phase LLM engine, which ensures deterministic reproducibility for reported quantitative results.

items.title: Position: Hippocampal Explicit Memory Is the Cornerstone for AGI
items.summary: Advancing Large Language Models toward Artificial General Intelligence requires integrating explicit memory capabilities. Current LLMs primarily function using an implicit memory mechanism, which is analogous to human learning but insufficient for complex cognition. Higher-order functions critical for AGI, such as strategic planning and metacognition, fundamentally depend on hippocampal explicit memory. This paper argues that purely statistical learning cannot support these advanced cognitive abilities. Therefore, researchers must focus on developing computational systems capable of artificial explicit memory integration.

items.title: Can AI Agents Synthesize Scientific Conclusions?
items.summary: Scientific AI agents are increasingly tasked with synthesizing conclusions from diverse evidence, a capability questioned in high-stakes areas like health. To address this, the authors introduce SciConBench, a large benchmark using expert-written systematic review questions to test open-domain scientific conclusion synthesis. They also created SciConHarness, a controlled evaluation harness designed to prevent data leakage during testing. Evaluations of frontier models revealed low factual quality, with the best agent achieving an F1 score of only 0.337 under clean-room conditions. Furthermore, consumer-facing agents were found to frequently produce incomplete or contradictory conclusions, highlighting that reliable scientific synthesis remains a significant open challenge.

items.title: Knowing When to Ask: Self-Gated Clarification for Hierarchical Language Agents
items.summary: Failures in hierarchical reasoning often stem from premature commitment due to missing intermediate information. ACTION-RATING reformulates clarification by integrating it directly into the agent's action space alongside navigation steps. This framework yields two distinct self-generated information-seeking modes: mandatory when no path is viable, and opportunistic despite a leading candidate. Testing on HTS classification showed an increase in Information-Seeking Effectiveness (ISE) from 50% to 74% through this mechanism. Furthermore, the patterns of seeking help remained stable even when answer quality was intentionally degraded.

items.title: Automated Mediator for Human Negotiation: Pre-Mediation via a Structured LLM Pipeline
items.summary: An automated mediator for human negotiation is introduced as a structured pipeline of specialized LLM modules designed for preparatory pre-mediation. This pipeline decomposes preparation into distinct stages: dialogue support, preference prediction, response critique, and summarization. Evaluating the system in controlled experiments showed that it achieves preparation outcomes comparable to professional human mediators on short-term self-reported metrics, such as trust and confidence. Specifically, the AI mediator exhibited a 36% lower Root Mean Square Error on preference inference compared to human baselines for the scenario tested. These results suggest structured LLM pipelines offer scalable pre-mediation support approximating human performance without requiring autonomous module interaction.

items.title: INFRAMIND: Infrastructure-Aware Multi-Agent Orchestration
items.summary: Existing multi-agent LLM orchestration methods fail to account for real-time serving infrastructure states, leading to resource bottlenecks on shared GPU clusters. INFRAMIND introduces a framework that integrates system awareness into the entire multi-agent stack. An infra-aware planner uses real-time load data to select topologies, favoring simpler graphs when congestion occurs. An infra-aware executor monitors queue depths and cache utilization at every step to guide model selection. By treating this as a hierarchical constrained MDP solved with reinforcement learning, INFRAMIND achieves superior performance, demonstrating up to +7.6 pp accuracy improvement while maintaining SLO compliance under high load.

items.title: Forecasting Future Behavior as a Learning Task
items.summary: Forecasting the behavior of large reasoning models (LRMs) is challenging because explanations derived from single token generations fail to generalize across long output trajectories. To address this, the authors propose training specialized Behavior Forecasters that predict future behavior directly from a single reasoning trajectory, bypassing explicit explanation steps. The forecasters are trained using unannotated queries on the LRM and perform inference with a single forward pass. Evaluation across two tasks—predicting answer repetition and input perturbation effects—showed these models outperformed GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus-4.6 readers on comparable trajectories while requiring significantly less computational cost. These findings demonstrate that the inherent information within the reasoning trajectory contains predictive power beyond simple human-style reading.

items.title: Search Discipline for Long-Horizon Research Agents
items.summary: Autoresearch agents often use an aggregate metric to select scientific candidates across diverse regions or cohorts. This aggregation can mislead selection because true validity may reside in the disaggregated structure, not the headline number. The paper demonstrates this failure using a fire-model task where the highest global scorer actually damaged protected boreal regions compared to a slightly lower-scoring alternative. To correct this, authors propose moving decision-making to an external control loop that audits candidates based on their per-region behavior rather than relying solely on the agent's score. This new "search-discipline protocol" prioritizes reviewing concrete candidate effects over optimizing the aggregate score.

items.title: SkillJuror: Measuring How Agent Skill Organization Changes Runtime Behavior
items.summary: Existing benchmarks inadequately measure how the organization of agent skills influences runtime behavior, overlooking the distinction between skill content and structure. SkillJuror introduces a framework to evaluate different skill writing paradigms using semantically controlled variants across multiple trials with fixed task knowledge. An 82-task SkillsBench study demonstrated that Progressive Disclosure significantly increased both distinct resources touched (from 1.18 to 3.85) and effective uptake events (from 1.33 to 3.92) compared to a flat baseline. This improvement resulted in more verifier-passing trials, although benefits were task-dependent. Findings confirm that skill organization actively alters how agents apply procedural knowledge, with gains most pronounced when resources guide implementation or repair.

items.title: Restless bandits with imperfect binary feedback: PCL-indexability analysis and computation
items.summary: Addresses restless bandits with binary latent states and imperfect binary feedback, relevant to spectrum access with sensing errors. Develops a Partial Conservation Laws (PCL)-based framework for analyzing and calculating indexability and the Whittle index using belief-state models. This framework analyzes stochastic dynamics via deterministic skeletons and renewal decompositions, yielding tractable expressions in several threshold regimes. For other regimes lacking full analytical verification, it derives efficient numerical schemes to compute marginal metrics and the marginal productivity (MP) index. Computational experiments strongly suggest that these conditions hold broadly, showing the MP index policy often outperforms standard benchmark policies.

items.title: To Intervene or Not: Guiding Inference-time Alignment with Probabilistic Model Blending
items.summary: Safe and effective use of large language models necessitates inference-time alignment due to their widespread deployment. Current methods often lack proper assessment of guidance reliability, leading to excessive interventions from ineffective suggestions. A novel framework called BlendIn addresses this by moving beyond binary decisions to create hybrid distributions incorporating knowledge from multiple models. BlendIn stabilizes alignment through quality-aware techniques, proportionally weighting model contributions based on measured reliability. This approach preserves useful guidance while minimizing the impact of unreliable suggestions, achieving performance improvements up to 50% on difficult model pairings.

items.title: Dual-Stance Evaluation of Sycophancy: The Structure of Agreement and the Limits of Intervention
items.summary: Dual-stance evaluation was introduced to test if efforts to reduce sycophancy in Large Language Models (LLMs) also suppress agreement with factual information. Applying this method using centroid-difference steering on Llama-3-8B-Instruct revealed a dissociation between the subspaces representing sycophantic and factual agreement. The specific steering direction equally affects both types of agreement, leading to reduced factually correct statements alongside less sycophancy. This suggests that while behavioral divergence exists, the current steering mechanism cannot selectively target either bias. Ultimately, the results highlight a general limitation where activation-readable representations may not be practically writable through mere manipulation of activations.

items.title: Few-Shot Resampling for Scalable Statistically-Sound Data Mining
items.summary: Assessing the statistical significance of data mining results often requires computationally expensive resampling methods unsuitable for large datasets. The paper introduces FewRS, a novel and efficient resampling-based technique to evaluate these results with rigorous guarantees against false discoveries. FewRS achieves this by utilizing a new bound on the supremum deviation of test statistics related to data mining quality. This approach drastically reduces the number of required resampled datasets while maintaining high statistical power across various applications like pattern and network mining. Consequently, it offers a scalable method for statistically validating results on large-scale real-world data, achieving up to two orders of magnitude speedup over existing state-of-the-art techniques.

items.title: ProHiFlo: Hierarchical Flow Matching with Functional Guidance for De Novo Protein Generation
items.summary: ProHiFlo is a novel hierarchical flow matching framework designed for advanced *de novo* protein generation. It incorporates coarse-to-fine generation, first modeling backbone geometry before refining details to all-atom coordinates for efficiency. The method includes functional guidance, which steers the generative process toward desired properties using pre-trained predictors without requiring retraining. Furthermore, it utilizes an adaptive SE(3)-equivariant architecture for efficient multi-scale processing. Experiments show ProHiFlo achieves state-of-the-art results, notably reaching a 58.9% success rate in enzyme active site scaffolding compared to previous models.

items.title: Physics-informed generative AI for semiconductor manufacturing: Enforcing hard physical constraints in generative models by construction
items.summary: Generative AI's use in semiconductor manufacturing requires designs like masks and process recipes to adhere strictly to hard physical constraints, making simple plausibility insufficient. This research posits that generative models for such constrained domains must be physics-informed by construction rather than relying solely on post-hoc filtering. The work surveys advanced architectural tools, including physics-informed diffusion and PDE-constrained variational models. It details four integration patterns connecting these generators with established physics simulators like TCAD and lithography solvers. Ultimately, the authors advocate for developing benchmarks and differentiable simulator infrastructure to ensure physical validity is inherent in AI design processes.

items.title: Bernstein-Schur Kernels: Random Features by Sketched Modulation and Radial Randomization
items.summary: Introduces a novel random feature construction for Bernstein-Schur kernels, which are products of finite-feature and completely monotone shift-invariant kernels. The method achieves this by simultaneously sketching the finite modulation factor and applying Gaussian random Fourier features to the one-dimensional Bernstein-Widder scale of the radial factor. This results in a feature dimension $Dm$, avoiding the $O(d^2)$ complexity of exact modulation features. The framework provides analytical guarantees, including unbiasedness and operator-norm bounds controlled by intrinsic dimension rather than maximum entries. An example application is to the biased $yat$-kernel, where the method utilizes an IMQ spectral sampler for the radial mixture component.

items.title: Loss Landscape Diagnosis for Gradient-Based Gray-Scott System Inversion: Disentangling the Roles of PINN Components
items.summary: Direct backpropagation through the Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion PDE structure for parameter recovery reveals an optimization landscape plagued by flat plateaus and sharp cliffs near bifurcation boundaries. Analyzing this setup without neural network components shows the residual loss alone results in a quadratic, smooth landscape, suggesting it inherently handles the full PDE dynamics across initial conditions. When the neural network is included, it proves incapable of fixing ill-posed parameter subspaces and only functions to complete observed data points. This research explicitly disentangles the roles of PINN components, suggesting distinct functionalities for each part of the modeling structure. The findings offer concrete guidance for designing improved Physics-Informed Neural Network methods generally.

items.title: PermDoRA -- Understanding Adapter Interference in Language Models: Limits of Parameter-Space Geometry
items.summary: Effective access control in large language models necessitates modular methods to prevent cross-domain interference without retraining. Researchers tested the hypothesis that linear overlap causes adaptation interference using a framework called DoRA-RBAC on LLaMA-3.1-8B and Mistral-7B across various QA benchmarks. Comparing standard Euclidean merging with geometry-aware, Riemannian-inspired averaging showed no consistent performance benefit for the latter in multi-domain settings. The study concludes that adapter interference is likely due to interactions within shared nonlinear representations rather than solely being governed by parameter-space geometry like orthogonality.

items.title: Annealed Entropic Allocation for Ranking and Selection
items.summary: Introduces Annealed Entropic Allocation, a weighted soft-min framework for sequential budget allocation in ranking and selection. It replaces the non-smooth maximin large-deviation rate objective with a weighted log-sum-exp surrogate that handles near-active challengers better. The method incorporates a saddlepoint approximation to enhance finite-budget discrimination using sub-exponential corrections. This surrogate maintains the same first-order target as the classical maximin formulation because the smoothing parameter is annealed to zero. Experiments show competitive performance, particularly when multiple challenger scores are closely grouped.

items.title: Enhancing Spectral Embedding through Robust and Flexible Knowledge Transfer in Electronic Health Records
items.summary: Presents a spectral-based, unsupervised learning framework for creating low-dimensional embeddings of clinical concepts and patients from limited electronic health records for rare diseases. To address data scarcity, it integrates a knowledge matrix derived from a larger population with partial overlap to the target cohort. Uniquely, the method abandons strict one-to-one signal alignment, permitting flexible structural knowledge sharing. It employs a novel two-step spectral embedding process: first cleaning irrelevant components from the knowledge matrix, then projecting to separate shared and heterogeneous information. Testing on multiple sclerosis data demonstrates superior performance compared to existing methods, especially when shared signals are weak or partially aligned.

items.title: Renewable Lasso without Batch-Number Constraints: A Gradient-Enhanced Approach
items.summary: Proposes a gradient-enhanced surrogate loss for online estimation of high-dimensional generalized linear models using streaming data. For non-distributed settings, this method improves upon prior renewable estimation techniques by removing the batch-number constraint. It extends to distributed streaming data in a master-client architecture, exchanging only summary gradient vectors. The adjusted approach avoids requiring clients to compute the full surrogate loss. Derived non-asymptotic error bounds validate the method's performance without strict limitations on batch count, showing improved accuracy over existing estimators.

items.title: Conformal Bayes under Label Shift: Post-Hoc Calibration vs. In-Training Adaptation
items.summary: Conformal Bayes integrates Bayesian posteriors with conformal calibration to generate statistically valid and efficient prediction sets. When addressing label shift, the work analyzes two methods: post-hoc calibration and in-training adaptation. Post-hoc calibration corrects the conformal threshold using importance weights while keeping the parameter posterior fixed. In contrast, in-training adaptation adjusts the entire parameter posterior towards the target domain. Experiments demonstrate that both strategies yield valid coverage under unbiased training, but in a lead-optimization scenario, in-training adaptation debiases the model by narrowing interval width without losing coverage.

items.title: From Persistence to Survival: Hypothesis Testing, Effect Sizes and Vectorisation for Topological Features
items.summary: Persistence diagrams, key tools in topological data analysis, lack natural vector space representations for comparative statistics. A novel approach called STRAND treats collections of these diagrams as survival data, using the persistence survival function $S(t) = \mathbb{P}(p > t)$ as its core metric. From this single representation, the method derives a non-parametric two-sample test and interpretable effect sizes for hypothesis testing. Furthermore, it generates a 1-Wasserstein-stable feature vector suitable for machine learning applications. Validation across synthetic data and benchmarks in graph and 3D point clouds demonstrates STRAND as a unified framework for topological analysis.

items.title: Phase Transitions in Attention: A Bayesian Theory of Copy Head Emergence
items.summary: Attention, central to in-context learning in transformers, often shows patterns emerging abruptly during training. Researchers present a Bayesian theory of feature learning within attention, specifically examining how a copy subcircuit emerges in an induction head's first layer. By deriving a closed-form posterior for the attention matrix, they reduce the problem to a low-dimensional parameter space. This reveals a phase transition in the required amount of training data, verified through both Bayesian sampling and standard Adam training. The findings contrast softmax attention exhibiting a first-order phase transition with linear attention's initial second-order transition followed by smooth evolution.

items.title: Fixed-Parameter Tractability of Private Synthetic Data Generation
items.summary: Fixed-parameter tractability (FPT) is established for generating synthetic data while satisfying differential privacy constraints. The specific parameter driving the tractability is the treewidth of the query family's incidence graph. Two distinct methods achieve optimal error rates: one utilizes linear programming and the FPT of the associated LP dual, and the other employs a subsampled private multiplicative weights method for Gibbs distribution sampling. Both algorithmic approaches are unified through a dynamic programming framework built over a tree decomposition.

items.title: Quantized Stochastic Primal-Dual Methods for Distributed Optimization under Relaxed Global Geometry
items.summary: Presents a quantized stochastic primal-dual method, q-PDGD, for distributed optimization involving stochastic gradients and finite-bit communication via random quantization. Analyzes this method under relaxed global geometry conditions. Under restricted secant inequality, constant step sizes guarantee linear contraction to a neighborhood dependent on noise and quantization error. With diminishing step sizes, the method achieves $O(1/k)$ convergence even without requiring shared minimizers. The proposed rates match state-of-the-art centralized stochastic rates in terms of oracle complexity.

items.title: GraphGP: Scalable Gaussian Processes with Vecchia's Approximation
items.summary: Gaussian processes are powerful but computationally costly due to their $\mathcal{O}(N^3)$ time complexity. GraphGP addresses this by implementing Vecchia's approximation, which conditions each point only on its $k$ nearest neighbors using a sparse precision matrix. This GPU algorithm achieves linear time and memory scaling, enabling use with very large datasets. Key technical advancements include a bit-reversed k-d tree ordering for efficient neighbor searches and maximizing batch parallelism. The method provides necessary building blocks for inference tasks like forward generation and log-determinant calculation via a differentiable CUDA implementation.

items.title: Signed Compression Progress on a Sealed Audit is Goodhart-Resistant
items.summary: Signed compression progress serves as a theoretically sound intrinsic motivation reward for agents improving their world model predictions. If this reward is defined by the signed decrease in a fixed, sealed-audit loss ($\text{r}_t = E(\theta_{t-1}) - E(\theta_t)$), cumulative rewards precisely track true audit improvement. For finite panels, empirical reward is bounded by true audit improvement plus a term related to model class deviation. This method proves robust against various failure modes, including clipping or scoring on the agent's own data stream. Overall, signed compression progress provides an accounting signal guaranteeing genuine performance enhancement when audited by a sealed panel.
